question.text: A retirement adviser can have tie-ups with multiple Points of Presence. Which condition must be met?

answer.text: Maximum of 3 PoP tie-ups are allowed
The interests of subscribers must not be adversely affected
Only PoPs from the same bank group are allowed
Prior PFRDA approval is required for each tie-up

explanation.text: **The Logic:** There is no restriction on the number of PoPs with whom the retirement adviser may enter into arrangements, provided that the interests of subscribers are not adversely affected. **The Trap:** There is no numerical cap (Option A is wrong) and no restriction to same bank group (Option C is wrong). The only condition is subscriber interest protection. **Cross-Exam Context:** Multiple intermediary relationships are common across financial services. Similar flexibility exists in SEBI's framework for distributors. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Multiple PoP tie-ups give you flexibility to serve subscribers better. Choose PoPs based on service quality, charges, and geographic coverage that benefits your subscribers.
